# Cost Forecast — 2026-06-17

**Subscription:** Azure subscription 1 (`ae5e40cd-96ef-48d8-950a-2e22cf8f991a`)
**Currency:** GBP
**Baseline window:** 2026-06-15 → 2026-06-16 (2 full post-cutover days) + 14-day component run-rates
**Generated by:** /cost-forecast

---

## Headline

All windows start today (2026-06-17) to match Azure's forecast API windows. Bottom-up = today's partial actual (£1.17) + remaining days at the £1.75/day run-rate.

| Horizon          | Window            | Bottom-up forecast | Azure forecast | Delta     | Confidence |
|------------------|-------------------|-------------------:|---------------:|----------:|------------|
| Rest of month    | 06-17 → 06-30 (14d) | £23.92           | £16.34         | **+£7.58 (+46%)** | High   |
| Next 30 days     | 06-17 → 07-16 (30d) | £51.92           | £34.63         | **+£17.29 (+50%)** | Medium |
| Next quarter     | 06-17 → 09-15 (91d) | £158.67          | £119.96        | **+£38.71 (+32%)** | Low    |

**TL;DR — Azure is *under*-forecasting by 32–50%.** The daily run-rate stepped up from ~£1.02/day (April), through ~£1.15/day in early June, to **~£1.75–1.82/day** from mid-June onward. The jump is the **go-live configuration landing**: a permanently-warm Go prod replica (`minReplicas=1`, +£0.25/day always-on), active Service Bus polling (+£0.09/day), and steadily-rising Cosmos RU consumption (+£0.26/day, £1.02 → £1.28). None of these are one-offs. Azure's trailing-average model is dragged down by the cheap first half of June (when prod was scale-to-zero and polling was quiet) and cannot fit the mid-month step-change, so it lands ~£1.17/day — about a third too low. The Azure forecast API is also **internally unstable**: the same June-remainder window returned £16.34, £13.52, and £5.91 across three calls, and July swung from £21.11 to £74.48 — a direct symptom of trying to extrapolate through a month of infra churn. Bottom line: budget for **~£52/month**, not Azure's ~£35.

---

## Why Azure's forecast is wrong

- **Go-live warm replica — the main driver Azure misses (+£0.25/day, ~£7.5/mo, always-on).** The new `ca-town-crier-api-go-prod` runs `minReplicas=1` (warm, confirmed in config) and has cost a steady **£0.25/day** since the cutover (06-15: £0.247, 06-16: £0.252). The .NET prod app it replaced was scale-to-zero and cost **£0 on idle days** (June 1–9 show zero Container Apps spend). So a continuous cost appeared mid-month that simply did not exist in the trailing window Azure averages over. This is a deliberate, accepted go-live decision (kill cold-start) — see *Recommendations* for why it is **not** flagged for reversal.

- **Cosmos is rising, not flat (+£0.26/day over the month).** `cosmos-town-crier-shared` climbed from **£1.02/day (06-02) to £1.28/day (06-16)**, tracking a rise in measured consumption from ~46 RU/s to ~54 RU/s. Azure's average of the whole month (~£0.97/day for the trailing 30d) sits below the *current* level and keeps falling behind as the line drifts up.

- **Active polling ramped (+£0.09/day).** `job-tc-poll-prod` went from negligible in early June to a steady **£0.09/day** (06-16: £0.089). Event-triggered Service Bus polling is now continuously busy.

- **Partially offsetting (pushes Azure's number *up*, but not enough): the .NET → Go cutover overlap.** Old `ca-town-crier-api-prod` and `-dev` ran *alongside* the new Go apps from 06-10 to 06-14, adding **£1.62** to June MTD as a one-off cutover cost (peaking 06-14 at £2.20/day total). Azure extrapolates a slice of that double-running forward; it should be excluded entirely (both .NET apps hit £0 from 06-15). This offsets — but does not cancel — the under-forecast from the three drivers above.

- **Verified change-points in the actuals timeline:**

  | Date       | Commit / event                                  | Effect on run-rate                                  |
  |------------|-------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------|
  | 2026-06-10 | `8120a3f9` Go dev app added; cutover testing begins | Container Apps line wakes from £0; .NET+Go overlap starts |
  | 2026-06-14 | `621714c2` Go prod bound + warmed; `.NET` apps stop | Cutover peak £2.20/day (one-off); .NET → £0 from 06-15 |
  | 2026-06-15 | First clean Go-only steady state                | £1.82/day total (the real forward run-rate)         |
  | (ongoing)  | Cosmos RU drift                                 | £1.02 → £1.28/day over June                          |

  The .NET decommission (Phase 3, complete 2026-06-15) removed `ca-town-crier-api-prod`/`-dev` from the forward run-rate; they are excluded from the bottom-up sum despite still appearing in June MTD.

## Spend by service (last 30 days)

_Period: 2026-05-19 → 2026-06-17 vs prior period 2026-04-18 → 2026-05-18._

| Service              | Last 30d  | Prior 30d | Δ vs prior |
|----------------------|----------:|----------:|-----------:|
| Azure Cosmos DB      | £29.05    | £29.79    | −£0.74     |
| Azure Container Apps | £4.18     | £3.49     | +£0.69     |
| Container Registry   | £3.67     | £3.90     | −£0.23     |
| Azure Monitor        | £0.11     | £0.03     | +£0.08     |
| Email (ACS)          | £0.02     | £0.02     | −£0.00     |
| Service Bus          | £0.00     | £0.00     | £0.00      |
| Log Analytics        | £0.00     | £1.74     | −£1.74     |
| **Total**            | **£37.03**| **£38.97**| **−£1.94** |

> ⚠️ **These two 30-day totals being roughly equal is itself the trap.** Both windows *average* ~£1.2–1.3/day, but the last-30d window blends cheap pre-go-live days (~£1.0/day) with the new ~£1.8/day run-rate. The **exit rate** (mid-June) is far above either window's mean — which is exactly why a trailing-window forecast under-shoots. Cosmos dominates at **~78%** of spend.

## Bottom-up breakdown by resource

_Run-rate basis: fixed-SKU and stable resources use the 14-day mean (06-03 → 06-16); the new Go apps and `poll-prod` use the clean post-cutover steady state (06-15 → 06-16). Cosmos central estimate £1.20/day sits between its 14-day mean (£1.07) and its current level (£1.28), reflecting the upward drift._

| Resource                     | Service              | Daily run-rate | Rest-of-month* | Notes                                                                 |
|------------------------------|----------------------|---------------:|---------------:|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| cosmos-town-crier-shared     | Azure Cosmos DB      | £1.200         | £15.60         | ~50 RU/s and **rising**. Serverless. 78% of the bill. See rec. tc-tm5o. |
| ca-town-crier-api-go-prod    | Azure Container Apps | £0.249         | £3.24          | **Warm** (`minReplicas=1`). New always-on cost since go-live.          |
| acrtowncriershared           | Container Registry   | £0.124         | £1.61          | Fixed Basic SKU daily fee. Only 75 MB / 10 GB — overage gone (was 148%). |
| job-tc-poll-prod             | Azure Container Apps | £0.097         | £1.26          | Event-triggered Service Bus polling; now continuously active.          |
| ca-town-crier-api-go-dev     | Azure Container Apps | £0.046         | £0.60          | Scale-to-zero (`minReplicas=0`); cost is deploy churn. 22 revisions → tc-fxau. |
| job-tc-poll-bootstrap-prod   | Azure Container Apps | £0.009         | £0.12          | 30-min schedule; no-op when queue healthy.                            |
| job-tc-digest-hourly-prod    | Azure Container Apps | £0.006         | £0.08          | Hourly cron.                                                          |
| job-tc-digest-hourly-dev     | Azure Container Apps | £0.005         | £0.07          | Hourly cron.                                                          |
| log-town-crier-shared        | Log Analytics        | £0.004         | £0.05          | Near-zero. Basic-Logs + 30d + 1 GB cap. Within free grant.            |
| (Azure Monitor — alerts)     | Azure Monitor        | £0.004         | £0.05          | Smart Detection / activity-log alerts.                                |
| job-tc-dormant-cleanup-prod  | Azure Container Apps | £0.002         | £0.03          | Daily cron.                                                          |
| acs-town-crier-uk            | Email                | £0.001         | £0.01          | Minimal email send cost.                                              |
| other tiny jobs + Service Bus| mixed                | £0.003         | £0.04          | digest-prod/dev, dormant-dev, sb-town-crier-prod — all near-zero.     |
| **Total**                    |                      | **£1.750**     | **£22.76**     | + £1.17 today (partial actual) → £23.92 for the 06-17→06-30 window.    |

\* Rest-of-month here = 13 forward days (06-18 → 06-30) at run-rate. Adding today's partial actual reconciles to the £23.92 headline.

**Excluded from the forward run-rate** (present in June MTD but decommissioned): `ca-town-crier-api-prod` (.NET, £0 from 06-15), `ca-town-crier-api-dev` (.NET, £0 from 06-15), `acs-town-crier-shared` (legacy Europe ACS, deleted 05-02), `job-tc-poll-safety-net-prod`, `job-tc-poll-dev` (renamed/removed in April). Both Static Web Apps (`swa-town-crier-dev`/`-prod`) are **Free SKU — £0**.

---

## Reconciliation (Pulumi vs reality)

`/infra` was ported C#/.NET → Go on 2026-06-17 (`221a84c5`); resource graph unchanged. Reconciliation done against the live `az resource list` (29 resources across `rg-town-crier-dev`/`-prod`/`-shared`) as ground truth:

- **No drift / no orphans.** Every billed resource maps to a Town Crier resource group and a known purpose. No portal-created or leftover resources.
- **Untagged (5):** two managed certificates (`cert-api-dev`/`-prod`) and three Azure-auto-created monitor objects (Smart Detection action group, `azureapp-auto`, Service Health alert). All are system-generated and free — expected, no action.
- **Revision sprawl:** `ca-town-crier-api-go-dev` has **22 active revisions** (`Multiple` mode); prod correctly runs `Single` (1 revision). Cost impact ~£0 (dev scales to zero) but it walks toward the 100-revision deploy ceiling → **tc-fxau**.
- **Prior recommendations resolved:** the April ACR purge worked (75 MB vs the 15.95 GB / 148%-over state in the 2026-04-25 report), and the App* tables now read 30-day retention (the 90d discrepancy is gone). Both prior beads are closed/compacted.

---

## Recommendations

### 1. Evaluate Cosmos cost reduction (throughput model + write efficiency)

- **Estimated saving:** ~£10–20/month (uncertain; carries throttling risk)
- **Bead:** `tc-tm5o`
- **Rationale:** Cosmos is **78% of the bill** (~£36/mo) at ~50 RU/s average and rising, driven mainly by polling upserts against a pre-revenue user base. Two levers: **(a)** a database-level **shared autoscale** throughput (e.g. 1000 RU/s max) could plausibly land at ~£10–15/mo since average usage sits near the 10% autoscale floor — but needs a polling-burst profile first (spikes above the cap throttle, and the app has previously fought serverless 429s) and a check that all containers can share throughput; **(b)** ~50 RU/s *continuous* with no real users implies redundant re-upserts of unchanged planning records each poll cycle — cutting that demand is the more durable fix. Quantified-but-uncertain and risk-bearing against the go-live reliability posture, hence P3.

### 2. Cap dev container-app inactive revisions (deploy-ceiling hygiene)

- **Estimated saving:** ~£0/month (reliability, not cost)
- **Bead:** `tc-fxau`
- **Rationale:** `ca-town-crier-api-go-dev` has 22 active revisions and climbs by one per deploy; Container Apps hard-caps at 100, after which deploys fail. Zero-effort one-line Pulumi fix: switch dev to `activeRevisionsMode=Single` (as prod) or set `maxInactiveRevisions=5`. Filed despite the ~£0 saving because it is zero-effort and prevents a future dev-deploy outage.

### Noted but deliberately NOT recommended

- **Warm Go prod replica (`minReplicas=1`, ~£7.5/mo)** is the largest *controllable* line, but it is an accepted go-live decision to eliminate cold-start latency (ref `project_aca_min_replicas_decision`, set 2026-06-13). Reverting it would trade UX for ~£7.5/mo and contradicts the pre-revenue posture of prioritising reliability. Left as-is.
- **ACR Basic (£3.7/mo)**, **Service Bus Basic (£0)**, **Log Analytics (£0.12/mo)** are all already at their floors. No action.

---

## Methodology footnote

- **Actuals:** Cost Management query API, 60-day window (2026-04-18 → 2026-06-17), daily granularity, grouped by `ResourceId` and `ServiceName`. 503 + 370 rows; no pagination. (The bundled `fetch_costs.sh` flatten step erred in this environment; raw `az rest` responses were flattened inline with the same jq logic.)
- **Run-rate:** Fixed/stable resources use the 14-day mean (06-03 → 06-16); new Go apps + `poll-prod` use the clean post-cutover days (06-15 → 06-16), the only complete days with Go as sole API and the .NET apps gone. Cosmos central £1.20/day reflects the upward drift between its 14-day mean (£1.07) and current level (£1.28). 06-14 (cutover peak) excluded as a one-off.
- **Azure forecast source:** Cost Management forecast API, Custom timeframe, Monthly granularity, three separate windows from 2026-06-17. The API returned internally-inconsistent results across windows (June-remainder £5.91–£16.34; July £21.11–£74.48) — documented above as evidence of model instability under churn.
- **Cosmos consumption:** `az monitor metrics` `TotalRequestUnits` (Total, P1D) 06-10 → 06-16: 3.0–4.6M RU/day, ~35–54 RU/s.
- **Pulumi reconciliation:** done against `az resource list` ground truth (stack export not separately run; resource graph unchanged by the Go port). No drift identified.
- **Exclusions / known gaps:** decommissioned .NET apps and renamed/removed April jobs excluded from forward projection (listed above). Next-quarter assumes the current run-rate plus continued Cosmos drift but **zero net-new user traffic** — real go-live adoption would push Cosmos (serverless) higher, so the £158.67 quarter figure is a **floor**, not a ceiling. Low confidence accordingly.
